welcomeMessage.en: Today is all about getting comfortable.
welcomeMessage.zh: 今天的主题是让你感到放松自在。

welcomeMessage.en: Don't worry about making mistakes—the goal today is just to get used to speaking English out loud.
welcomeMessage.zh: 不要担心犯错——今天的目标只是让你习惯大声说英语。

welcomeMessage.en: We are going to talk about where you grew up and your favorite childhood memories.
welcomeMessage.zh: 我们将谈论你长大的地方和你最喜欢的童年回忆。

questionsToThinkAbout.en: How would you describe the place where you grew up to someone who has never been there?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 你会如何向从未去过那里的人描述你长大的地方？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: What was your absolute favorite thing to do when you were 10 years old?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 当你10岁的时候，你最喜欢做的事情是什么？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: Who was your childhood best friend, and what did you do together?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 你童年最好的朋友是谁，你们一起做过什么？

vocabulary.example: "I used to play outside every day."
"The people in my hometown are very friendly."

sessionSummary: Kiki shared what it is like living in Nanchang, discussing the hot summers, mildly spicy rice noodles, and local landmarks like the Tengwang Pavilion and the 303 skyscraper.
Yating talked about Fuzhou, the beautiful "Blue Tears" natural phenomenon in Pingtan, and local sweet and sour seafood dishes like lychee pork.

keyVocabulary.example: "Pronounced exactly like 'muscle' (肌肉), but spelled differently!"
"We use these terms instead of saying 'little lobster'."
"The Blue Tears in Pingtan are a beautiful natural phenomenon."
"The 303 building in Nanchang is a skyscraper."
"You can get a free ticket if you recite the Tengwang Pavilion poem."
"I like to eat crayfish, but I don't like peeling them."
"My dad gave me a ride to school."
"We park the car in the underground parking lot."

grammarTips.title: Talking About the Past
grammarTips.description: When sharing childhood memories, always remember to switch your verbs to the past tense.

grammarTips.title: "When I was a child..."
grammarTips.description: Make sure not to drop the "a".

grammarTips.title: Getting to School
grammarTips.correct: "My friend gave me a ride on their bike."
